article_id: Preamble
text_snippet: Preamble WE, THE PEOPLE OF THE REPUBLIC OF THE MARSHALL ISLANDS, trusting in God, the Giver of our life, liberty, identity and our inherent rights, do hereby exercise these rights and establish for ou

article_id: Section 1. Executive Authority and Collective Responsibility of the Cabinet
text_snippet: 1. The executive authority of the Republic of the Marshall Islands shall be vested in the Cabinet, whose members are collectively responsible to the Nitijela. 2. Subject to law, the Cabinet may exerci

article_id: Section 1. Existing Law to Continue
text_snippet: 1. Subject to this Constitution the existing law shall, until repealed or revoked, and subject to any amendment thereof, continue in force on and after the effective date of this Constitution; all rig

article_id: Section 1. Freedom of Thought, Speech, Assembly, Association, and Petition
text_snippet: 1. Every person has the right to freedom of thought, conscience, and belief; to freedom of speech and of the press; to the free exercise of religion; to freedom of peaceful assembly and association; a

article_id: Section 1. Legislative Control of Public Revenue and Expenditure
text_snippet: 1. No taxes shall be imposed or other revenue raised and no public money shall be expanded unless authorized by law. 2. All revenues received by the Government of Republic of the Marshall Islands shal

article_id: Section 1. Legislative Power Vested in the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. The legislative power of the Republic of the Marshall Islands shall be vested in the Nitijela and shall be exercised by Act. 2. The power conferred by this Section shall include the power to repeal

article_id: Section 1. Persons Becoming Citizens
text_snippet: 1. A person who, immediately before the date of this Constitution, was a citizen of the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands shall on that date become a citizen of the Republic of the Marshall Islan

article_id: Section 1. Power to Amend the Constitution
text_snippet: Any amendment of this Constitution shall become law only pursuant to this Article.

article_id: Section 1. Right to a System of Local Government
text_snippet: 1. The people of every populated atoll or island that is not part of an atoll shall have the right to a system of local government which shall operate in accordance with any applicable law. 2. The sys

article_id: Section 1. The Council of Iroij
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Council of Iroij of the Republic of the Marshall Islands. 2. The Council of Iroij shall consist of 5 eligible persons from districts of the Ralik Chain and 7 eligible persons from

article_id: Section 1. The Judicial Power
text_snippet: 1. The judicial power of the Republic of the Marshall Islands shall be independent of the legislative and executive powers and shall be vested in a Supreme Court, a High Court, a Traditional Rights Co

article_id: Section 1. The Public Service of the Republic of the Marshall Islands
text_snippet: 1. The Public Service of the Republic of the Marshall Islands shall comprise all such employees as may be necessary to assist the Cabinet in exercising the executive authority of the Republic and to p

article_id: Section 1. This Constitution to be Supreme Law
text_snippet: 1. This Constitution shall be the supreme law of the Republic of the Marshall Islands; and all judges and other public officers shall be bound thereby. 2. No legislative or executive instrument and no

article_id: Section 1. Traditional Rights of Land Tenure Preserved
text_snippet: 1. Nothing in Article II shall be construed to invalidate the customary law or any traditional practice concerning land tenure or any related matter in any part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands

article_id: Section 10. Appointments within the Public Service
text_snippet: 1. All employees of the Public Service shall be appointed by or under the authority of the Public Service Commission and, subject to any law, shall hold office on such conditions as may from time to t

article_id: Section 10. Imprisonment for Debt
text_snippet: No person shall be imprisoned for debt; nor shall any person be imprisoned for failure to pay a fine assessed as punishment for a crime unless he has been afforded a reasonable time to make payment an

article_id: Section 10. Lapse of Appropriation
text_snippet: The appropriations made by the Appropriation Act or any Supplementary Appropriation Act shall lapse at the end of the financial year to which that Act relates, or at the end of such longer period as t

article_id: Section 10. Meetings of the Cabinet
text_snippet: 1. No business shall be transacted at any meeting of the Cabinet unless at least 4 members of the Cabinet are present. 2. The Cabinet shall not be disqualified from the transaction of business only be

article_id: Section 10. Privileges of the Council of Iroij and its Members
text_snippet: 1. Neither the Council of Iroij nor any member of the Council shall be subject to any proceeding outside that body, or subjected to any liability, civil or criminal, in relation to the casting of any

article_id: Section 10. Sessions of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. The Nitijela shall meet in regular session on the first Monday in January in each year and shall, subject to Section 11 of this Article, remain in session for 50 sitting days; Provided that the Pre

article_id: Section 11. Clerk of the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Clerk of the Council of Iroij who shall be an officer of the Public Service and shall have the functions conferred on him by this Constitution or by or pursuant to Act or to a reso

article_id: Section 11. Compensation of Certain Officers Charged on the General Fund
text_snippet: 1. The compensation payable to the holders of the offices of judge of the Supreme Court or of the High Court, of member of the Public Service Commission and of Auditor-General shall be a charge on the

article_id: Section 11. Conditions of Employment in the Public Service
text_snippet: In establishing and revising the conditions of employment in the Public Service, the factors to be taken into account shall include a. the need for the Public Service to recruit and retain an efficien

article_id: Section 11. Conscription and Conscientious Objection
text_snippet: No person shall be conscripted to serve in the armed forces of the Republic of the Marshall Islands except in time of war or imminent danger of war as certified by the Cabinet, and no person shall be

article_id: Section 11. Instruments and Other Decisions Made by the Cabinet
text_snippet: 1. Any instrument made by the Cabinet shall have effect, and any other decision of the Cabinet shall be duly authenticated, when that instrument or the record of that decision has been signed by the P

article_id: Section 11. Special Provisions as to Termination of Sessions and Recesses of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. Subject to Section 12 and 13 of this Article, whenever, during any session of the Nitijela, an election of a President is required, that session of the Nitijela shall not terminate and no recess sh

article_id: Section 12. Clerk of the Cabinet
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Clerk of the Cabinet who shall be an officer of the Public Service and shall be responsible for arranging the business for and keeping the minutes of meetings of the Cabinet, and f

article_id: Section 12. Dissolution of the Nitijela and General Election
text_snippet: 1. Pursuant to paragraphs (2) and (3) of this Section, the Nitijela shall be dissolved and there shall be a general election of all members of the Nitijela in every fourth calendar year, unless the Ni

article_id: Section 12. Equal Protection and Freedom from Discrimination
text_snippet: 1. All persons are equal under the law and are entitled to the equal protection of the laws. 2. No law and no executive or judicial action shall, either expressly, or in its practical application, dis

article_id: Section 12. Public Debt Charged on the General Fund
text_snippet: All debt charges for which the Republic of the Marshall Islands is liable shall be a charge on the General Fund.

article_id: Section 13. Personal Autonomy and Privacy
text_snippet: All persons shall be free from unreasonable interference in personal choices that do not injure others and from unreasonable intrusions into their privacy.

article_id: Section 13. Special Provision for Early Dissolution of the Nitijela and a General Election
text_snippet: 1. The President may, by writing signed by him, dissolve the Nitijela if a motion of no confidence in the Cabinet has twice been carried out and has twice lapsed, and no other President has held offic

article_id: Section 13. The Auditor-General
text_snippet: 1. The Speaker shall nominate and, with the approval of the Nitijela, signified by resolution, the President shall appoint an Auditor-General of the Republic of the Marshall Islands. 2. The Auditor-Ge

article_id: Section 14. Access to Judicial and Electoral Processes
text_snippet: 1. Every person has the right to invoke the judicial process as a means of vindicating any interest preserved or created by the law, subject only to regulations which limit access to courts on a non-d

article_id: Section 14. Clerk of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Clerk of the Nitijela who shall be an officer of the Public Service and shall have the functions conferred on him by this Constitution or by or pursuant to Act or to the Rules or a

article_id: Section 14. Compensation of Auditor-General
text_snippet: The compensation of the Auditor-General shall be specifically prescribed by Act.

article_id: Section 15. Audit of Accounts
text_snippet: 1. The Auditor-General shall audit the public funds and accounts of the Republic of the Marshall Islands including those of all Departments or offices of the legislative, executive and judicial branch

article_id: Section 15. Health, Education, and Legal Services
text_snippet: The Government of the Republic of the Marshall Islands recognizes the right of the people to health care, education, and legal services and the obligation to take every step reasonable and necessary t

article_id: Section 15. Procedure of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. Subject to this Section and to any Act, the Nitijela may from time to time make Rules for the regulation and orderly conduct of its proceedings and the dispatch of its official business. 2. The Rul

article_id: Section 16. Ethical Government
text_snippet: The Government of the Republic of the Marshall Islands recognizes the right of the people to responsible and ethical government and the obligation to take every step reasonable and necessary to conduc

article_id: Section 16. Privileges of the Nitijela and its Members
text_snippet: 1. The validity of any proceeding in the Nitijela or in any committee thereof, or in any joint conference or joint committee, and the validity of any certificate duly given by the Speaker under Sectio

article_id: Section 17. Compensation of the Members of the Nitijela
text_snippet: The compensation of the President, the Ministers, the Speaker, the Vice-Speaker and the other members of the Nitijela shall be specifically prescribed by Act.

article_id: Section 17. Other Rights
text_snippet: The enumeration in this Constitution of certain rights shall not be construed to deny or disparage others retained by the people.

article_id: Section 18. Introduction of Bills in the Nitijela
text_snippet: Any member of the Nitijela may introduce any Bill, or propose any motion for debate in, or present any petition to, the Nitijela; and any such Bill, motion or petition shall be considered and disposed

article_id: Section 18. Invoking Bill of Rights Protection
text_snippet: 1. No right secured by the Bill of Rights may be denied or abridged, whether directly through the imposition of force or penalty, or indirectly through the withholding of privilege or benefit. 2. Any

article_id: Section 19. Special Provisions with Regard to Bills Prescribing Compensation
text_snippet: 1. This Section shall apply to any Bill or amendment to a Bill which, in the opinion of the Speaker, prescribes the compensation of any person or class of persons whose compensation is required to be

article_id: Section 2. Classification of Amendments
text_snippet: 1. Any amendment of this Article, or of Articles I, II, or X, or of the principles of apportionment set forth in paragraph (4) of Section 2 of Article IV, or any amendment abolishing or altering in an

article_id: Section 2. Composition of the Cabinet
text_snippet: 1. The Cabinet shall consist of the President (who shall be a member of the Nitijela) and the other members of the Nitijela who are appointed as Ministers pursuant to this Article. 2. Subject to Secti

article_id: Section 2. Declaration of the Customary Law
text_snippet: 1. In the exercise of its legislative functions, it shall be the responsibility of the Nitijela, whenever and to the extent considered appropriate, to declare, by Act, the customary law in the Republi

article_id: Section 2. Functions of the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: The Council of Iroij shall have the following functions: a. the Council may consider any matter of concern to the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and it may express its opinion thereon to the Cabine

article_id: Section 2. Inconsistency with this Constitution
text_snippet: 1. Any existing law and any law made on or after the effective date of this Constitution, which is inconsistent with this Constitution shall, to the extent of the inconsistency, be void. 2. Any other

article_id: Section 2. Membership of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. The Nitijela shall consist of 33 members to be elected from the following electoral districts in the number indicated beside the name of each electoral district: Majuro 5 Kwajalein 3 Ailinglaplap 2

article_id: Section 2. Municipal Councils
text_snippet: Every Municipal Council, whether chartered or not, existing immediately before the effective date of this Constitution shall be a local government for the purpose of Article IX.

article_id: Section 2. Persons Performing the Functions of an Office
text_snippet: A reference to the holder of any office provided for in this Constitution includes any person who, for the time being, is performing the functions of that office pursuant to law.

article_id: Section 2. Persons who may be Registered as Citizens
text_snippet: 1. Unless disqualified pursuant to paragraph (3) of this Section, any person who is not a citizen of the Republic of the Marshall Islands shall become a citizen by registration if, upon application, t

article_id: Section 2. Power to Make Ordinances
text_snippet: 1. A local government may make ordinances for the area in respect of which it has jurisdiction, provided that such ordinances are not inconsistent with any Act, or, to the extent that it has the force

article_id: Section 2. Slavery and Involuntary Servitude
text_snippet: 1. No person shall be held in slavery or involuntary servitude, nor shall any person be required to perform forced or compulsory labor. 2. For the purpose of this Section, the term "forced or compulso

article_id: Section 2. The Cabinet to Take Responsibility for Budgetary Matters
text_snippet: 1. It shall be the responsibility of the Cabinet to make proposals to the Nitijela on all matters pertaining to the budget. 2. Except with the recommendation or consent of a member of the Cabinet, the

article_id: Section 2. The Chief Secretary
text_snippet: 1. There shall be an officer of the Public Service to be called the Chief Secretary, who shall be the head of the Public Service and the chief administrative and advisory officer of the Government of

article_id: Section 2. The Supreme Court
text_snippet: 1. The Supreme Court shall be a superior court of record, shall consist of a Chief Justice and such number of other judges as may from time to time be prescribed by Act, and shall have appellate juris

article_id: Section 20. Special Provisions with Regard to Bills Prescribing the Qualifications of Judges
text_snippet: The Nitijela shall not proceed further than the first reading of any Bill or amendment to a Bill which, in the opinion of the Speaker, prescribes any qualification required for appointment as a judge,

article_id: Section 21. When Bills Become Law
text_snippet: 1. Subject to the requirements of paragraph (5) of Section 4 of Article XII, in those cases to which that paragraph applies, a Bill shall become law if, and only if it has been passed by the Nitijela;

article_id: Section 3. Amendment by Action of the Nitijela and Referendum
text_snippet: Amendments of this Constitution made pursuant to this Section shall originate in the Nitijela, and, subject to this Section, shall be considered and disposed of as if they had been proposed by Bill. A

article_id: Section 3. Determination of Quorum and Right to Vote
text_snippet: In any case where this Constitution prescribes the number of the members of any body which shall constitute a quorum, that number shall include the member of that body who is for the time being presid

article_id: Section 3. Elections of Members of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. Elections of members of the Nitijela shall be conducted by secret ballot under a system of universal suffrage for all citizens of the Republic of the Marshall Islands who have attained the age of 1

article_id: Section 3. Interpretation and Application of this Constitution
text_snippet: 1. In interpreting and applying this Constitution, a court shall look to the decisions of the courts of other countries having constitutions similar, in the relevant respect, to the Constitution of th

article_id: Section 3. Powers of the Nitijela Regarding Citizenship
text_snippet: The Nitijela may make provision by Act a. for the acquisition of citizenship of the Republic of the Marshall Islands by registration in cases not falling within Section 2 of this Article; b. for the a

article_id: Section 3. Requests for Reconsideration of Bills
text_snippet: 1. Subject to paragraph (8) of this Section, the Clerk of the Nitijela shall transmit to the Clerk of the Council of Iroij, for reference to the Council, a copy of every Bill adopted on third reading

article_id: Section 3. The Attorney General
text_snippet: 1. There shall be an Attorney General who shall be an officer of the Public Service and shall have the same qualifications as those required for appointment as a judge of the High Court. 2. The Attorn

article_id: Section 3. The General Fund
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Republic of the Marshall Islands General Fund. 2. All taxes and other revenues and moneys raised or received by the Government of the Republic shall be paid into the General Fund,

article_id: Section 3. The High Court
text_snippet: 1. The High Court shall be a superior court of record having general jurisdiction over controversies of law and fact in the Republic of the Marshall Islands; shall consist of a Chief Justice, and such

article_id: Section 3. The President
text_snippet: 1. The President shall be the Head of State of the Republic of the Marshall Islands. 2. The President shall be elected by a majority of the total membership of the Nitijela and shall be appointed to o

article_id: Section 3. Transition to Government under this Constitution
text_snippet: Notwithstanding any other provision in this Constitution, and only for so long as their terms require, this Constitution shall have effect subject to any transitional provisions either made as describ

article_id: Section 3. Unreasonable Search and Seizure
text_snippet: 1. The right of the people to be secure in their persons, houses, papers and effects, against unreasonable searches and seizures, shall not be violated, and no warrants shall issue, but upon probable

article_id: Section 4. Amendment by Constitutional Convention and Referendum
text_snippet: 1. Amendments of this Constitution made pursuant to this Section shall be valid for all intents and purposes as part of this Constitution if duly certified by the Speaker as having been submitted to t

article_id: Section 4. Appointment of the Cabinet after Election of the President
text_snippet: 1. As soon as practicable after his election to that office, the President elect shall nominate to the Speaker for appointment as Ministers not less than 6 nor more than 10 other members of the Nitije

article_id: Section 4. Compensation of Members of the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: The compensation of members of the Council of Iroij shall be specifically prescribed by Act.

article_id: Section 4. Conformity with the Trusteeship Agreement
text_snippet: Notwithstanding any other provision in this Constitution, and only for so long as the Trusteeship Agreement extends to the Republic of the Marshall Islands as part of the law of the Republic, this Con

article_id: Section 4. Dates, Days and Periods of Time
text_snippet: 1. In any case where this Constitution prescribes a date, or a means of fixing a day, or a period of time, for the performance of any duty or for the happening of any event or for any other purpose th

article_id: Section 4. Due Process and Fair Trial
text_snippet: 1. No person shall be deprived of life, liberty, or property without due process of the law. 2. Every person charged with a criminal offense shall be presumed innocent until proven guilty beyond a rea

article_id: Section 4. Enforcement of this Constitution
text_snippet: Subject to the Constitution's limitations on the judicial power, a. The Attorney General acting in the name of the people of the Republic of the Marshall Islands, and all persons directly affected by

article_id: Section 4. Qualifications of Candidates
text_snippet: 1. Every qualified voter who has attained the age of 21 years is qualified to be a candidate for election as a member of the Nitijela. 2. Any person who is qualified to be a candidate under paragraph

article_id: Section 4. The Secretary of Finance
text_snippet: 1. There shall be an officer of the Public Service to be called the Secretary of Finance who shall be the head of the Finance Department. 2. In addition to the other functions and powers conferred on

article_id: Section 4. The Traditional Rights Court
text_snippet: 1. The Traditional Rights Court shall be a court of record; shall consist of panels of 3 or more judges selected so as to include a fair representation of all classes of land rights, including, where

article_id: Section 4. Withdrawals from the General Fund or Other Public Account
text_snippet: 1. No moneys shall be withdrawn from the General Fund unless the issue of those moneys has been authorized by the Appropriation Act or a Supplementary Appropriation Act; or has been authorized as anti

article_id: Section 5. Allocation of Portfolios
text_snippet: 1. The President shall, as soon as practicable after taking office, by writing signed by him, allocate among the members of the Cabinet (including himself if he so desires) the portfolios of Minister

article_id: Section 5. Authentic Text
text_snippet: The Marshallese and English texts of this Constitution shall be equally authentic, but in case of difference, the Marshallese text shall prevail.

article_id: Section 5. Certification by the Speaker
text_snippet: Whenever a certificate of the Speaker is required by this Article as a precondition of the validity of an amendment, the Speaker shall give such a certificate upon being satisfied that the amendment h

article_id: Section 5. Just Compensation
text_snippet: 1. No land right or other private property may be taken unless a law authorizes such taking; and any such taking must be by the Government of the Republic of the Marshall Islands, for public use, and

article_id: Section 5. Provisions made by the Republic of the Marshall Islands Nitijela
text_snippet: For all or any of the purposes referred to in Section 3 or Section 4 of this Article, the applicable transitional provisions shall be those made, before the effective date of this Constitution, by Act

article_id: Section 5. Public Servants who Become Candidates or are Elected
text_snippet: 1. Employees of the Public Service who become candidates for election as members of the Nitijela shall be granted leave of absence for the purposes of their candidature in accordance with any conditio

article_id: Section 5. The Cabinet to Supervise Expenditure and to Account to the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. No public money shall be expended without the approval of the Cabinet or of a person or body to which such approval authority has been delegated by or pursuant to [an] Act. 2. Any such delegation,

article_id: Section 5. The Chairman and the Vice-Chairman of the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: 1. The Chairman and the Vice-Chairman of the Council of Iroij shall be the members of the Council elected to those offices by a majority of the members present and voting at a meeting of the Council.

article_id: Section 5. The Judicial Service Commission
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Judicial Service Commission which shall consist of a Chairman, the Chief Justice of the High Court, or if his office is vacant, a person qualified by legal training, experience and

article_id: Section 5. The Public Service Commission
text_snippet: 1. There shall be a Public Service Commission of the Republic of the Marshall Islands consisting of a Chairman and 2 other members. 2. The Chairman and the other members of the Public Service Commissi

article_id: Section 6. Appointment of Ministers and Allocation of Portfolios at other Times
text_snippet: 1. The President may, at any time when the total number of Ministers is less than 10, submit to the Speaker, with the consent of the member, the nomination of a member of the Nitijela for appointment

article_id: Section 6. Cruel and Unusual Punishment
text_snippet: 1. No crime under the law of the Republic of the Marshall Islands may be punishable by death. 2. No sentence of imprisonment at hard labor shall be imposed on any person who has not attained the age o

article_id: Section 6. Duty to Report
text_snippet: It shall be the duty of the Nitijela to make provision, at least once every ten years, for a report on the advisability of amending this Constitution, or of calling, or holding a referendum on the que

article_id: Section 6. Effective Date of this Constitution
text_snippet: Subject to the prior approval of this Constitution by a majority of the votes validly cast in a referendum, the effective date of this Constitution shall be May 1, 1979.

article_id: Section 6. Functions of the Chairman
text_snippet: 1. The Chairman shall preside over any meeting of the Council of Iroij at which he is present and shall have the other functions conferred on him by this Constitution or by or pursuant to Act or to a

article_id: Section 6. Implementation of a Compact of Free Association with the United States
text_snippet: For the purpose of achieving consistency between this Constitution and any provision of a Compact of Free Association between the Government of the Republic of the Marshall Islands and the Government

article_id: Section 6. Tenure of Office of Members of the Public Service Commission
text_snippet: A member of the Public Service Commission may at any time resign his office by writing signed by him, addressed to the President; he shall not be removed or suspended from office except on the like gr

article_id: Section 6. The Annual Appropriation Bill
text_snippet: 1. The Minister of Finance shall, in relation to each financial year, lay before the Nitijela, as soon as practicable after the commencement of its regular session, budget estimates of the revenues an

article_id: Section 6. Vacation of Seats by Members of the Nitijela
text_snippet: 1. The seat of any member of the Nitijela shall become vacant if, and only if, he ceases to possess any qualification to be a candidate which he was required to have at the time of his candidature; or

article_id: Section 7. Anticipated and Reprogrammed Expenditures
text_snippet: 1. Subject to such restrictions as may be prescribed by Act, the Cabinet may approve the expenditure of such sums as it considers necessary in anticipation of provision to be made in the Appropriation

article_id: Section 7. Compensation of the Members of the Public Service Commission
text_snippet: The compensation of the Chairman and other members of the Public Service Commission shall be specifically prescribed by Act.

article_id: Section 7. Habeas Corpus
text_snippet: 1. In order that the legality of any person's detention always remain subject to appropriate challenge in a court of law, the writ of habeas corpus shall not be suspended. 2. Any person held in custod

article_id: Section 7. Procedure of the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: 1. The Council of Iroij shall meet in regular session during any period when the Nitijela is meeting in regular session, and in special session during any period when the Nitijela is meeting in specia

article_id: Section 7. Residual Responsibility of the Constitutional Convention
text_snippet: If at any time before the effective date of this Constitution transitional provisions for all or any of the purposes referred to in Section 3 of this Article are adopted by or pursuant to a resolution

article_id: Section 7. The Speaker and the Vice-Speaker
text_snippet: 1. The Speaker and the Vice-Speaker shall be the members of the Nitijela elected to those offices by the Nitijela. 2. The Nitijela shall, by secret ballot, proceed to elect the Speaker and the Vice-Sp

article_id: Section 7. Vote of No Confidence in the Cabinet
text_snippet: 1. At any meeting of the Nitijela, any 4 or more members of the Nitijela who are not members of the Cabinet may give notice of their intention to make a motion of no confidence in the Cabinet. 2. Any

article_id: Section 8. Ex Post Facto Laws and Bills of Attainder
text_snippet: 1. No person shall be subjected to ex post facto punishment--such as punishment in excess of that validly applicable at the time the act in question was committed, or punishment imposed by a procedure

article_id: Section 8. Functions of the Speaker
text_snippet: 1. The Speaker shall preside over any meeting of the Nitijela at which he is present and shall have the other functions conferred on him by this Constitution or by or pursuant to Act or to the Rules o

article_id: Section 8. Procedure of the Public Service Commission
text_snippet: 1. At least 2 members of the Public Service Commission shall concur in any decision of the Commission. 2. Subject to this Section and to any Act, the Public Service Commission shall determine its own

article_id: Section 8. Supplementary Appropriation Bills
text_snippet: 1. If, after the passing of the Appropriation Act in respect of any financial year, the cabinet finds it necessary or desirable to propose any expenditure over and above that authorized by that Approp

article_id: Section 8. Vacancies in the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: 1. The seat of a member of the Council of Iroij shall become vacant if he dies; or he resigns his seat by writing under his hand delivered to the Clerk of the Council; or he ceases to be a qualified v

article_id: Section 8. Vacation of Office by Ministers
text_snippet: 1. A Minister shall vacate his office as a member of the Cabinet if his appointment as a Minister is revoked by the President, by instrument signed by him; or he vacates his seat in the Nitijela for a

article_id: Section 9. Acting President
text_snippet: 1. Whenever, by reason of illness, or absence from the Republic of the Marshall Islands or from the seat of government, or for any other reason, the President is temporarily prevented from performing

article_id: Section 9. Contingencies Fund
text_snippet: 1. If so empowered by Act, the Cabinet, on being satisfied that there has arisen an urgent and unforeseen need for expenditure for which no other provision exists, may authorize advances from the Gene

article_id: Section 9. Deputies of Members of the Council of Iroij
text_snippet: 1. A member of the Council of Iroij who is prevented by absence, illness or any other cause from attending any meeting of the Council or of any committee thereof or of any joint committee or joint con

article_id: Section 9. Determination on Questions of Membership of the Nitijela
text_snippet: Any questions that arise concerning the right of any person to vote at an election of a member or members of the Nitijela, or to be or to remain a member of the Nitijela, or to exercise the rights of

article_id: Section 9. Functions and Powers of the Public Service Commission
text_snippet: 1. The Public Service Commission shall be the employing authority for the Public Service and shall have the general oversight and control of its organization and management and shall be responsible fo

article_id: Section 9. Quartering of Soldiers
text_snippet: No soldier shall, in time of peace, be quartered in any house without the consent of the owner, nor in time of war but in the manner prescribed by law.
